_meta.purpose: Endocrinology / hormone reference intervals + axis interpretation for the rules engine + LLM. Many hormones need paired axis interpretation (e.g. TSH+FT4, ACTH+cortisol) and timing (diurnal, cycle day, fasting). Decision support only; endocrinologist confirms with dynamic testing.
_meta.source: Standard endocrine reference intervals + guideline cut-offs. Curated for JORINOVA NEXUS; confirm assay-specific ranges.

tsh.name: TSH
tsh.note: First-line. High TSH+low FT4 = primary hypothyroid; low TSH+high FT4 = hyperthyroid; low TSH+low FT4 = central.

cortisol_am.name: Cortisol (9am)
cortisol_am.note: <100 am with symptoms -> adrenal insufficiency (Short Synacthen); >dynamic suppression fails -> Cushing.

acth.name: ACTH
acth.note: High ACTH+low cortisol = primary (Addison); low ACTH+low cortisol = secondary.

aldosterone.name: Aldosterone
aldosterone.note: Use aldosterone:renin ratio for primary hyperaldosteronism (Conn).

prolactin.name: Prolactin
prolactin.note: High -> prolactinoma, drugs, pregnancy; exclude macroprolactin.

gh.name: Growth hormone
gh.note: Interpret with IGF-1 + dynamic tests (OGTT suppression / GST).

pth.name: Parathyroid hormone (PTH)
pth.note: Interpret WITH calcium: high Ca+high/normal PTH = primary hyperPTH; low Ca+high PTH = secondary; high Ca+low PTH = malignancy.

fsh.name: FSH
fsh.note: Cycle/sex dependent; high FSH+LH -> primary gonadal failure/menopause.

testosterone.name: Testosterone (total, male)
testosterone.note: Morning fasting sample; interpret with SHBG/free T.

insulin.name: Insulin (fasting)
insulin.note: Interpret with paired glucose + C-peptide (hypoglycaemia workup).
